free_text: Proton and deuteron induced reactions on natGa:
Experimental and calculated excitation functions
Targets withstanding high beam power were developed
using electrodeposition of Ga/Ni alloy (70/30%) on
thick Cu backings.
The cross sections
were measured using a stacked foil irradiation
followed by non-destructive g-ray spectrometry. The
targets were Ga-Ni alloy (70/30%) deposited with the
electroplating technique on Au (thickness 25 um, for
exp2) and on Cu (12.5 um, for exp1 and exp3)
foils as backing.
In the stack irradiated with 50 MeV deuterons at LLN,
22 Ga-Ni targets on Au backing were interleaved with
55 Al (98 lm and 49.6 lm) and 15 Ni (23.9 lm)
monitor/degrader foils.
The stack of exp1 consisting of 20 Ga-Ni alloy on Cu
backing targets, interleaved with Tl/Cu degraders was
irradiated with a 36 MeV proton beam at VUB. In this
experiment the Cu backings were used for monitoring.
In exp2 (65 MeV protons LLN, energy in last foil
46.9 MeV) 18 Ga-Ni targets on Au backing were
interleaved with Hf foils and 26.9 lm Al
degraders/monitors.
The stack of exp3 (65 MeV protons LLN) consisted of
20 Ga-Ni on Cu targets, interleaved with W and Au
foils, separated by 55 Al degrader/monitor foils of
250 or 125 lm. The median energy in the last Ga-Ni
target was 30.6 MeV.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT online data library.
Uncertainty on the energy scale includes
cumulative effects of initial beam energy, stopping
estimation influenced by target thickness and beam
straggling. Throughout the stack it increases from
0.2 MeV for the first foil to 1.2 MeV for proton
irradiation and 0.2 MeV for the first foil to 2 MeV for
deuteron irradiation.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time
parameters is not included. Overall uncertainty were
surface density of target nuclei(5%),
incident charged particle flux (5%),
nuclear decay and branching data (5%)
detector efficiency (5%)
statistical and peak fitting (2-8%)
MPT
VT+On. Revision in 008, 014, 016, 017, 019
subents.
